summary: Hot-fix retest of menu-item card height bug from iteration_10. Verified that the `auto-rows-min content-start` classes added to the grid in /entry/billing menu container fix the stretching issue. All 4 menu-item-* cards now render at 58.5px height (previously 654px), well under the 80px assertion threshold. Cards sit at the top (y=114) instead of stretching to fill the column.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Hot-fix retest only validated menu-item card height. Measurements: width=102.3px, height=58.5px, all 4 cards in grid at /entry/billing render at top (y=114). Grid container classes confirmed: 'grid grid-cols-3 sm:grid-cols-4 lg:grid-cols-6 xl:grid-cols-7 auto-rows-min content-start gap-1.5 overflow-y-auto flex-1 pb-2'. Bug is fixed.
rca of the issue: Root cause: The grid container had `flex-1` which made it absorb all vertical space. With CSS Grid's default `grid-auto-rows: auto`, when only 1 row of items existed, that single row stretched to fill the entire flex-1 height (~654px). Fix: Adding `auto-rows-min` constrains row height to content size, and `content-start` aligns rows at the top of the container so the remaining flex-1 space stays empty (scrollable) rather than expanding rows. Verified post-fix: card heights are now 58.5px, identical to designed compact size.
